volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 1.3.255d - Section 1.3.325)

sections.label: Section 1.3.255d
sections.original: Súrdus sum † profecto inanilogistae .
sections.translation: I ’ m deaf .

sections.label: Section 1.3.256
sections.original: Dedí dum fuít .
sections.translation: I gave you money so long as I had it .

sections.label: Section 1.3.256b
sections.original: Non petó quod dedísti .
sections.translation: I ’ m not asking what you gave .

sections.label: Section 1.3.257
sections.original: Dabó quando erít .
sections.translation: I ’ ll give you some when I have it .

sections.label: Section 1.3.257b
sections.original: Ducitó quando habébis .
sections.translation: When you have it , bring it to me [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.258
sections.original: éheu , quam ego malis pérdidi modis
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! In what a foolish fashion have I lavished what I brought to you , and what I gave you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.259b
sections.original: Mórtua
sections.translation: Your wealth defunct ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.260
sections.original: vérba re núnc facis ; stúltus es , rem áctam agis .
sections.translation: you now are talking about it ; you are a simpleton , a cause that has been tried you are trying over again .

sections.label: Section 1.3.261
sections.original: Nósce saltem húnc quis est .
sections.translation: At least consider him , who he is .

sections.label: Section 1.3.261b
sections.original: Iám diu scio
sections.translation: I ’ ve known for a long time now who he was ; who he now is , let him know himself . Do you walk on ( to the BOY ) .

sections.label: Section 1.3.263b
sections.original: Potin út semel modo ,
sections.translation: And can ’ t you , Ballio , only once give a look this way for your own profit ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.265
sections.original: Réspiciam istoc prétio ; nam si sácruficem summó Iovi
sections.translation: At that price I ’ ll give a look ; for if I were sacrificing to supreme Jupiter , and were presenting [*] the entrails in my hands to lay them on the altar , if in the meanwhile anything in the way of profit were offered , I should in preference forsake the sacrifice . There ’ s no being able to resist that sort of piety , however other things go .

sections.label: Section 1.3.269
sections.original: Deos quidem , quos maxume aequom est metuere , eos minimi facit .
sections.translation: The very Gods , whom it is especially our duty to reverence — them he esteems of little value .

sections.label: Section 1.3.270
sections.original: Compellabo . salve multum , serve Athenis pessume .
sections.translation: I ’ ll speak to him . Hail to you , right heartily , the very vilest slave in Athens .

sections.label: Section 1.3.271
sections.original: Di te deaeque ament vel huius arbitratu vel meo ,
sections.translation: May the Gods and Goddesses favour you , Ballio , both at his wish and at my own ; or , if you are deserving of other terms , let them neither favour nor bless you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.273
sections.original: Quid agitur , Calidore ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter , Calidorus ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.273b
sections.original: Amatur atque egetur acriter .
sections.translation: Love and pinching want [*] are the matter .

sections.label: Section 1.3.274
sections.original: Misereat , si familiam alere possim misericordia .
sections.translation: I would pity you , if , upon pity I could support my establishment .

sections.label: Section 1.3.275
sections.original: Heia , scimus nos quidem te qualis sis ; ne praedices .
sections.translation: Aye , aye , we know you quite well , what sort of character you are ; don ’ t be proclaiming it . But do you know what we want ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.276b
sections.original: Pol ego propemodum : ut male sit mihi .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I know it pretty nearly ; that there may be something unfortunate for me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.277
sections.original: Et id et hoc quod te revocamus . quaeso animum advorte .
sections.translation: Both to that and this for which we called you back , prithee do give your attention .

sections.label: Section 1.3.277b
sections.original: Audio .
sections.translation: I am attending ; but compress into a few words what you want , as I ’ m busy now .

sections.label: Section 1.3.279
sections.original: Hunc pudet , quod tibi promisit quaque id promisit die ,
sections.translation: He ( pointing to CALIDORUS ) is quite ashamed about what he promised you , and the day for which he promised it ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.280
sections.original: quia tibi minas viginti pró amica etiam non dedit .
sections.translation: that he hasn ’ t even yet paid you those twenty minae for his mistress .

sections.label: Section 1.3.281
sections.original: Nimio id quod pudet facilius fertur quam illud quod piget .
sections.translation: That which we are ashamed at is much more easily endured than that which we are vexed at . At not having paid the money , he is ashamed ; I , because I have not received it , am vexed .

sections.label: Section 1.3.283
sections.original: At dabit , parabit : aliquot hos dies manta modo .
sections.translation: Still , he ’ ll pay it , he ’ ll procure it ; do you only wait some days to come . But he has been afraid of this , that you ’ ll sell her on account of his embarrassment .

sections.label: Section 1.3.285
sections.original: Fuit occasio , si vellet , iam pridem argentum ut daret .
sections.translation: He had an opportunity , had he wished , of paying the money long ago .

sections.label: Section 1.3.286
sections.original: Quid , si non habui ?
sections.translation: What if I had it not ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.286b
sections.original: Si amabas , invenires mutuom ,
sections.translation: If you had been in love , you would have found it on loan . You would have gone to the usurer [*] ; you would have paid the interest ; or else you would have pilfered it from your father .

sections.label: Section 1.3.288b
sections.original: Surruperet hic patri , audacissume ?
sections.translation: Ought he to have pilfered it from his father , you most shameless villain ? There is no fear that you ’ ll point out to him anything that ’ s right .

sections.label: Section 1.3.289b
sections.original: Non lenoniumst .
sections.translation: That ’ s not like a procurer .

sections.label: Section 1.3.290
sections.original: Egon patri surrupere possim quicquam , tam cauto seni ?
sections.translation: And could I possibly pilfer anything from my father , an old man so much on his guard ? And besides , if I could do so , filial affection forbids .

sections.label: Section 1.3.291b
sections.original: Audio .
sections.translation: I understand you ; do you then at night embrace filial affection in place of Phœnicium . But since I see you prefer your filial affection to your love — are all men your fathers ? Is there no one for you to ask

sections.label: Section 1.3.295
sections.original: mutuom argentum ?
sections.translation: to lend you some money ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.295b
sections.original: Quin nomen quoque iam interiit mutuom .
sections.translation: Why , the very name of lending ’ s dead and gone by this .

sections.label: Section 1.3.296
sections.original: Heus tu , postquam hercle isti a mensa surgunt satis poti viri ,
sections.translation: Look you now ; since , i ’ faith [*] , those fellows arose from the banker ’ s table , with a filled skin , who , when they called in their own , paid what they had borrowed to no born creature , since then , I say , all people have been more cautious not to trust another .

sections.label: Section 1.3.299
sections.original: Nimis miser sum , nummum nusquam reperire argenti queo ;
sections.translation: Most wretched am I ; nowhere am I able to find a coin of silver ;

sections.label: Section 1.3.300
sections.original: ita miser et amore pereo et inopia argentaria .
sections.translation: so distractedly am I perishing both through love and want of money .

sections.label: Section 1.3.301
sections.original: Eme die caeca hercle olivom , id vendito oculata die :
sections.translation: Buy oil on credit [*] , and sell it for ready money ; then , i ’ faith , even two hundred minae ready money might be raised .

sections.label: Section 1.3.303
sections.original: Perii , annorum lex me perdit quinavicenaria .
sections.translation: There I ’ m done ; the twenty-five year old law [*] founders me . All are afraid to trust me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.304b
sections.original: Eadem est mihi lex : metuo credere .
sections.translation: The same law [*] have I . I ’ m afraid to trust you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.305
sections.original: Credere autem ? eho an paenitet te , quanto hic fuerit usui ?
sections.translation: To trust him , indeed ! How now , do you repent of the great profit he has been to you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.306
sections.original: Non est usu quisquam amator nisi qui perpetuat data ;
sections.translation: No lover is a profitable one , except him who keeps continually making presents . Either let him be always giving , or when he has nothing , let him at the same time cease to be in love .

sections.label: Section 1.3.308
sections.original: Nilne te miseret ?
sections.translation: And don ’ t you pity me at all ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.308b
sections.original: Inanis cedis , dicta non sonant .
sections.translation: You come empty-handed ; words don ’ t chink . But I wish you life and health .

sections.label: Section 1.3.309b
sections.original: Eho an iam mortuost ?
sections.translation: Heyday ! Is he dead already ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.310
sections.original: Vtut est , mihi quidem profecto cum istis dictis mortuost :
sections.translation: However he is , to me indeed , at all events , with these speeches , he is dead . Then , does a lover really live , when he comes begging to a procurer ? Do you always come to me with a complaint that brings [*] its money . As for that , which you are now lamenting about , that you have got no money , complain of it to your stepmother [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.314b
sections.original: Eho an umquam tu huius nupsisti patri ?
sections.translation: Why , have you ever been married to his father , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.315
sections.original: Di melius faciant .
sections.translation: May the Gods grant better things .

sections.label: Section 1.3.315b
sections.original: Face hoc quod te rogamus , Ballio ,
sections.translation: Do what we ask you , Ballio , on my credit , if you are afraid to trust him . Within the next three days , from some quarter , in some way , either by land or sea , I ’ ll rout up this money for you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.318
sections.original: Tibi ego credam ?
sections.translation: I , trust you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.318b
sections.original: Quor non ?
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.318c
sections.original: Quia pol quá opera credam tibi ,
sections.translation: Because , i ’ faith , on the same principle that I trust you , on that principle I should tie a run-away dog to a lamb ’ s fry .

sections.label: Section 1.3.320
sections.original: Sicine mi abs te bene merenti male refertur gratia ?
sections.translation: Is the obligation thus ungratefully returned by you to me , who have deserved so well of you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.321
sections.original: Quid nunc vis ?
sections.translation: What do you want now ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.321b
sections.original: Vt opperiare hos sex dies aliquos modo ,
sections.translation: That you will only wait these six days of the Feast , and will not sell her or prove the death of the person who loves her .

sections.label: Section 1.3.322b
sections.original: Animo bono es .
sections.translation: Be of good courage ; I ’ ll wait six months even .

sections.label: Section 1.3.323b
sections.original: Euge , homo lepidissume .
sections.translation: Capital — most delightful man !

sections.label: Section 1.3.324
sections.original: Immo vin etiam te faciam ex laeto laetantem magis ?
sections.translation: Aye ; and do you wish , too , that from joyful I should make you even more joyous ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.325
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: How so ?
